_note: Optional. scry works with zero config (built-in defaults). The canonical config lives once per computer at ~/.config/scry/config.json (run `scry init` to generate it); a project that needs a different panel can drop a ./scry.config.json beside its code (run `scry init --local`), which overrides the global config when scry runs from that directory. This repo's config.json is a reference/example — it is NOT auto-loaded by name; pass it with `--config ./config.json` to use it. CLI flags (--mode/--panel/--judge/--aggregator/--no-web/--effort/--max-tool-calls) override the config.

settings._note: Global default Fusion knobs; each pipeline phase can override them in the `phases` block below. web_tools enables web_search+web_fetch (the panel/judge default; synthesis is web-off via phases, mirroring OpenRouter Fusion). timeout is the per-call timeout in seconds (the default for every phase; `scry plan`'s final draft raises it via phases.final). effort/max_output_tokens/max_tool_calls applied only where a CLI supports them. save_history writes each run to ~/.scry for `scry last`/`scry log` (override per-run with --no-save); it is global, not per-phase. temperature has no CLI equivalent and is intentionally absent.

phases._note: Per-phase overrides of the settings above. Each stage inherits every global setting and overrides only what it lists. Resolution per call (later wins): settings -> phases[phase] -> the `final` overlay (plan draft only) -> CLI flags. Stages: panel/judge/synthesis (a normal run), interview (plan clarifying rounds), final (the plan's deep draft, applied across its panel/judge/synthesis). Overridable: web_tools, max_tool_calls, timeout, effort, max_output_tokens. e.g. to run the judge web-off with fewer turns: "judge": {"web_tools": false, "max_tool_calls": 4}.

check._note: Zero-cost pre-flight for `scry --check`. probe is a NON-billing command; verifies_auth=false means a pass only confirms install (claude has no cheap login check).
check.note: login isn't auto-verifiable — run /login (Pro/Max). Programmatic 'claude -p' draws from your monthly Agent SDK credit.

stream._note: Realtime output for the final synthesis only (token-by-token reveal). Used when stdout is a TTY and not --json/--quiet; falls back to buffered if unavailable.

usage._note: Real cost/token accounting. claude reports these in --output-format json and the stream-json 'result' event; dotted paths read the parsed object. Providers without a 'usage' block report nothing (not metered per call).

web_off: Bash Edit Write NotebookEdit WebFetch WebSearch

check.install: install Codex — npm i -g @openai/codex (see OpenAI docs)

agy._note: Google via the Antigravity CLI. Headless `agy -p "<prompt>"` takes the prompt as an ARG (prompt:arg + prompt_flag -p), not stdin, and prints plain text (capture:text). No system-prompt flag (folded into the prompt) and no effort flag. Web search works via Gemini grounding, ON BY DEFAULT (verified live) — there is no flag to toggle it, so caps is empty and --no-web cannot disable agy's grounding. Model is the full name from `agy models`, e.g. 'Gemini 3.1 Pro (High)'.

check.note: sign in via the Antigravity app; 'agy models' should then list models
check.install: install Google Antigravity and its 'agy' CLI

deepseek._note: API-KEY EXCEPTION (testing only). DeepSeek has no subscription CLI, so the scry-deepseek adapter (a sibling script resolved next to scry) calls DeepSeek's OpenAI-compatible API and needs DEEPSEEK_API_KEY. Deliberately NOT in the default panel — add via --panel '...,deepseek:deepseek-chat' or here. Knowledge-only (no web search). Set model to 'deepseek-v4-pro' for the Pro tier; the legacy 'deepseek-chat' / 'deepseek-reasoner' aliases route to V4-Flash (non-thinking / thinking).

check.note: the one API-key provider — set DEEPSEEK_API_KEY; knowledge-only (no web)
check.install: ships with scry as ./scry-deepseek; export DEEPSEEK_API_KEY

kimi._note: Moonshot via the Kimi CLI (`kimi`). Headless `kimi --quiet` runs print mode (text, final-message-only, auto-approve) and prints the final answer as plain text (capture:text); the prompt is on stdin. No per-call system flag (folded into the prompt) and no argv flag to allow/deny tools or toggle web — so scry hands kimi a generated read-only agent file (--agent-file) that inherits the built-in `default` agent minus the mutating tools, and (when web is off) minus the SearchWeb+FetchURL tools too. That makes kimi read-only like the other panels AND able to honor --no-web (unlike agy). Auth is `kimi login` (Kimi Code OAuth — reuses your membership, no API key); KIMI_API_KEY is unset so a stray key can't divert billing off the subscription.

check.note: log in for subscription auth:  kimi login  (Kimi Code OAuth — reuses your membership, no API key)
check.install: install Kimi CLI — curl -LsSf https://code.kimi.com/install.sh | bash  (or: uv tool install --python 3.13 kimi-cli)
